sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root נָדַב.-

(1) voluntary, willing, spontaneous, ready, 1Ch 28:21 more fully נְדִיב לִבֹּו “willing of heart,” Exo 35:5, 22 Exo 35:22 2Ch 29:31 (see נָדַב Kal and Hithp.); Psa 51:14, רוּחַ נְדִיבָה “a ready spirit.”

(2) giving spontaneously, i.e. liberal, Pro 19:6 hence-

(3) generous, noble (which, indeed, amongst the Orientals is closely connected with liberality in giving), used of character, Isa 32:5, Isa 32:8 Pro 17:7, 26 Pro 17:26. It is applied-

(4) to nobility of race, and is a subst., a prince, Job 34:18 Psa 107:40 113:8 118:9 Pro 25:7 1Sa 2:8 used even in a bad sense, a tyrant, Job 21:28 Isa 13:2, compare משְׁלִים.-In many of the significations this word agrees with its synonym נָגִיד; but their order is exactly contrary. This word, from the idea of readiness and liberality of mind, which it originally means, has been applied to nobility of race; נָגִיד, from the original idea of a leader and ruler, is applied to those virtues which become a prince.
